California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Henry s. Barbosa 
Barbosa & Vera 
Attorneys at Law 
May 19, 1989 
1000 Corporate Center Drive, suite 350 
Monterey Park, CA 
91754 
Dear Mr. Barbosa: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. G-89-249 
As city Attorney for the city of Montebello, you have 
requested our advice concerning the applicability of the mass 
mailing prohibition of the political Reform Act (the "Act")1 to an 
article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter. 
The Commission provides advice to persons whose duties under 
the Act are in question. 
(Section 83114.) 
As Ci~y Attorney for 
the city of Montebello, you may request informal assistance 
because you have the duty to advise other persons relating to 
their duties or actions under the Act. 
(Regulation 18329(c) (1), 
copy enclosed.) 
However, the Commission does not provide advice 
relating to past conduct. 
(Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A).) 
Your request deals with past conduct because you have asked 
us to determine whether an article which has already been 
published complies with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act. 
Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. 
If you 
believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our 
Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at 
(916) 322-6441. 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916)322,5660 


File No. G-89-249 
Page 2 
The Act prohibits certain types of mass mailings sent at 
public expense. 
(Section 89001.) 
A city newsletter may be 
subject to this prohibition. 
For your information and future 
reference we are enclosing a copy of Regulation 18901 which 
clarifies the prohibition in Section 89001. 
Should you have any further questions regarding the above, 
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Kathryn E. Donovan

been directed to refer to you a request that you review an 
article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter for Spring 
1989, for compliance under "Prop. 73." 
For your information, I 
have attached a copy of the specific article. 
Your prompt response will be~preciated.

Boulevard Closure Stops Cruising 
Thc cruising prohlcm that has plagucd Montebello since 1978 resur-
liKcd again in full force in January, and according to Deputy Police 
Chief Steve Simonian. it is the Police Department's highest priority 
to alleviate the gridlock. 
The prphlel1l reached a peak on January IS, but thanks to the quick 
action of the Police Department, Boulevard closures wcre enacted and 
lIlost of the prohlem has subsided. According to Chief Simonian, the 
only option open when the cruising problcm escalatcs is to cnact a 
filII Boulcvard closure. Whitticr Boulevard was closed starting at Maple

VOl. 3. NO. 2. SPRING 1989 
Council Calls for Special Election 
The Montebello City Council has 
voted to schedule a special election 
on May 2. 1989 to provide the 
voters an opportunity to approve or 
disapprove the ordinance that gives 
the Redevelopment Agency the 
power of eminent domain in two 
areas of Montebello. 
In November the Council ap-
proved the ordinances to give the 
City's Redevelopment Agency con-
demnation powers to spur rede-
velopment and improve its abilitv 
to bring land uses into conformanc~ 
with zoning laws. The ordinance in-
cluded a restriction that would pre-
vent the use of eminent domain on 
single family owner occupied 
houses. The power of eminent do-
main could not be used outside the 
Project Area boundaries. 
At another special meeting con-
ducted the following week, Coun-
cil directed that a third ballot 
measure be added to the election. 
The measure would require any fu-
ture extension of the power of emi-
nent domain over homes to be 
submitted to a vote of the people. 
A major point of contention 
heard from residents throughout the 
town hall meetings and public hear-
ings was concern regarding a future 
council being able to amend these 
ordinances to extend the powers of 
eminent domain over homes. The 
third ballot measure stems from the 
City Council's desire to reassure 
residents that they would be pro-
tected from the use of eminent do-
main both now and in the future. 
City officials are counting on fu-
ture development in the Project 
Areas to create jobs and generate 
needed tax revenue that provides 
such basic services as police and 
fire. Montebello is currently facing 
a mounting budget short fall to the 
tune of $1.4 million. The short fall 
was created by federal funding cut-
backs and other revenue reductions 
during times when the need for 
public services continues to in-
crease. 
Instead of increasing taxes or fur-
ther reducing services, the city had 
chosen to encourage new develop-
ment in an attempt to ensure the 
citls long term financial stability. 
OffiCials looked at eminent domain 
as a way to allow the city to be able 
to improve its ability to assemble 
land for better industrial uses. New 
development is anticipated to raise 
tax revenue for governmental serv-
ices and provide jobs for the city's 
residents. officials said. 
The city conducted an un-
precedented seven town hall meet-
ings during the month of September 
in an attempt to inform the residents 
and to gather feedback about the or-
dinances. Most residents located 
both in and near the project areas 
received a letter of invitation to at-
tend one of the scheduled meetings. 
In addition. the city formed the 
South Montebello Citizens Adviso-
ry Ad Hoc Committee. The pur-
pose of the Citizens Committee was 
to review and provide input to the 
proposed Redevelopment Plan 
Amendments. 
The Committee determined that 
more control in eliminating 
incompatible uses and addressing 
the problems of under utilized land 
and substandard uses existing with-
in the Project Areas was needed 
since the City was at a competitive 
disadvantage with other cities con-
cerning redevelopment efforts. 
The Committee further deter-
mined that a more "proactive" ap-
proach is needed to include land 
use controls and rigid industri-
al/commercial development and 
performance standards. The com-
mittee found that the amendment to 
the redevelopment plan would en-
courage improved streets, moving 
heavy industrial uses to the M-2 
zone. better utilities, more parking, 
and allow land assembly in order 
to retain desirable existing busi-
nesses by helping them expand, and 
to lessen existing negative impacts 
on residential areas and streets. 
For information on how to 
register to vote. call the City 
Clerk's office between 8 a.m. and 
5 p.m. at (213)725-1200, ext. 330 . 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Henry S. Barbosa 
City Attorney 
City of Montebello 
Barbosa & Vera 
April 27, 1989 
Los Angeles Corporate Center 
1000 Corporate Center Dr., suite 1000 
Monterey Park, CA 
91754 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-249 
Dear Mr. Barbosa: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on April 25, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact Blanca Breeze an attorney in the Legal Division, 
directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh 
Very truly yours,

KathrynVE. Donovan 
General Counsel 
f' , 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question: As city Attorney for the city of Montebello, you have requested our advice concerning the applicability of the mass mailing prohibition of the political Reform Act (the "Act") to an article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter.
sections.conclusion: Your request deals with past conduct because you have asked us to determine whether an article which has already been published complies with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act. Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.
sections.question_synthetic: Does an article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter comply with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because the request concerns past conduct (an already-published article), which falls outside the scope of informal advice under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A). The requestor may contact the Enforcement Division if they believe a violation has occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (April 20, 1989 letter from Barbosa) and FPPC response (May 19, 1989 letter from Donovan). Response is a declination letter based on past conduct limitation. Newsletter article included as enclosure to incoming request.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: As city Attorney for the city of Montebello, you have requested our advice concerning the applicability of the mass mailing prohibition of the political Reform Act (the "Act") to an article published in the Montebello Today Newsletter.

CONCLUSION: Your request deals with past conduct because you have asked us to determine whether an article which has already been published complies with the mass mailing prohibitions of the Act. Therefore, we are unable to advise you in this matter. If you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division at this address or by telephone at (916) 322-6441.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to advise on whether a published article in the Montebello Today Newsletter complied with Political Reform Act mass mailing prohibitions, as the request concerned past conduct which is outside the scope of informal advice. The FPPC provided regulatory guidance on mass mailing prohibitions for future reference.
